title: Bridge Essay: Bodily self-recognition in patients with pathological embodiment.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Birth/Body** (Phase 1) with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Links Birth/Body to mechanistic neuroscience through Bodily, self-recognition, in, patients, with.

**Bodily self-recognition in patients with pathological embodiment.** — J Neurosci Res. PMID: 35869668. DOI: 10.1002/jnr.25109. The ability to discriminate between one'sown and others' body parts can be lost after brain damage, as in patients who misidentify someone else'shand as their own (pathological embodiment). Surprisingly, these patients do not use visual information to discriminate between the own and the alien hand. We asked whether this impaired visual discrimination emerges only in the ecological evaluation when the pathological embodiment is triggered by the physical alien hand (the examiner'sone) or wheth
